source: A Bird in the Hand (The Design Mechanism, Monster Island adventure)
author: John Holmes
verification_notes: Vision-verified all spirit stats against Bird in Hand PDF p.43-46 page images. Corrected Whulla POW/CHA, filled all previously UNVERIFIED entries with actual PDF data.
purpose: Demonstrates how spirits are statted, described, and used in play. Provides concrete examples of spirit stat blocks at various intensities for use as templates in chargen.

spirit_stat_block_format.description: Each spirit has a name, type, intensity, characteristics, derived skills, and abilities. Also includes a narrative description of how the spirit manifests.

fields.name: Unique spirit name (e.g., Whulla, Jked, Ghu)

characteristics.INS_or_INT: INS for nature/animal spirits, INT for ancestor/sapient spirits
characteristics.POW: Determined by intensity per Mythras Core table

fields.abilities: One or more spirit abilities from Mythras Core list
fields.narrative: Description of how the spirit manifests and behaves

example_spirits.name: Whulla
example_spirits.narrative: Respected by the Oodaki for the speed with which it snares Huku spiders. When manifest it appears as if dozens of grey rodents scurry across Kaia's body, granting limbs greater alacrity.

example_spirits.name: Eous
abilities: Endowment (Camouflage — two difficult grades penalty to opponents attempting to spot spirit worshipper)
example_spirits.narrative: The sleepy spirit of the sloth. Summoned from its binding, Eous casts a sloth-shaped shadow over Kaia that lets his great bulk blend slowly into the background.

example_spirits.name: Anylil
example_spirits.narrative: The crimson butterfly. It manifests as a burst of vibrant red, and then settles onto Mele's tattooed hide, giving it a rouge-sheen and noticeably quicker reflexes.

example_spirits.name: Woeyff
abilities: Endowment (Camouflage — two difficult grades penalty to opponents attempting to spot spirit worshipper)
example_spirits.narrative: A gift of Oodaku, manifesting as a shadowed sprawl of eight octopoid limbs, barely discernible by the naked eye. Mele simply seems to fade from view when cloaked by Woeyff.

example_spirits.name: Jked
abilities: Endowment (Camouflage — two difficult grades penalty to opponents attempting to spot spirit worshipper)
example_spirits.narrative: Must be coaxed from its fetish with a hooting cry, seemingly answered in echo as Jked grants simian dexterity. Spirit opponents are pelted with a hail of foul excrement.

example_spirits.name: Kugkowcs
abilities: Bless (Stealth — improves by one difficult grade)
example_spirits.narrative: The alien spirit of the Tetrapus. Kugkowcs manifests by Mele seemingly vomiting it out, marking the path for Mele to tread with utmost quiet.

example_spirits.name: Hoja
abilities: Endowment (Grappler — a successful strike allows an immediate Grapple in addition to inflicting damage, or if Parrying, Grip or Pin Weapon)
example_spirits.narrative: Another blessing of Oodaku, Hoja is held in a hoop of twisting coral. When coaxed forth from its fetish, Hoja seemly transforms Mele's hands into writhing tentacles, grasping a target with spectral suckered-limbs.

example_spirits.name: Fylue
abilities: Endowment (Venomous — POT 72, Resist Endurance, Onset 1 Combat Round struck Hit Location then affecting rest of the body 1d3+3 rounds late, Duration 2d10 minutes, Condition Paralysis; may use Inject Venom Special Effect Mythras p.218)
example_spirits.narrative: The spirit of the river Eels found not far from Mele's village. Fylue's spectral bite injects a soothing liquid that drains the strength of its opponents.

example_spirits.name: Ghu
abilities: Absorb Magic — any magic cast at Mele causes Ghu to visibly appear, looming over Mele, and absorbing magic into its trunk
example_spirits.fetish: Smooth bark of a Ghua tree
example_spirits.narrative: The sheltering spirit of the Ghua tree. Its fetish is the smooth bark of a mighty specimen that has been revered by Mele's tribe for five generations. When manifest, Mele is cast in shadow.

tiki_spirits.description: Tiki Masks are fetishes carved to present a fearsome visage. They hold Intensity 1 or 2 nature spirits. Activation requires a Binding or Hard Willpower check as an action at the cost of 1 MP; the aid lasts for a single scene and the spirit departs, the fetish empty.
tiki_spirits.typical_blessing: +2 to a specific skill or attribute
tiki_spirits.note: These are one-use-per-scene spirits offered to adventurers — a good model for starting character spirits.

iz_unique_spirit.name: Iz
iz_unique_spirit.description: An alien entity, partly in the Spirit World and wholly inimical to life. The adventure's antagonist.
iz_unique_spirit.note: Not relevant for chargen but shows how high-intensity spirits are statted.

starting_spirit_worshipper.description: Based on the NPC Kaia (Spirit Worshipper rank, p.42-43), a starting animist character would have:
spirits: 1 Intensity 1 Nature Spirit (friendly, in fetish) — e.g. Whulla
1 Intensity 2 Nature Spirit (friendly, in fetish) — e.g. Eous
starting_spirit_worshipper.note: Kaia has exactly 2 spirits at Spirit Worshipper rank (CHA 14 / 2 = 7 slots, but only 2 bound).

spirit_generation_for_chargen.approach: For a starting Spirit Worshipper in Glorantha:
steps: 1. Determine bound spirit limit: CHA / 2 (Mythras Core p.136)
2. Assign 2-3 spirits from the cult's friendly spirit list
4. Roll or assign characteristics per Mythras Core intensity table (p.133)
5. Each spirit occupies 1 slot regardless of intensity (per Mythras Core)
6. Create fetish for each spirit (narrative description)

gaps: source page count is not recorded
no committed page/block evidence or verifier run metadata is available
